title: Life System Harmony

content.question: Where is your life most out of harmony?
sop: List your major life domains: business, health, relationships, home, finances, personal growth.
For each pair, ask whether one is currently being sacrificed to fuel another.
Circle the domain most consistently sacrificed, that's your active conflict point.
Count how many domains currently feel aligned versus total domains (see calculation, page 5), then redesign one weekly routine so the conflicting domains share resources instead of competing.
Re-audit monthly, harmony is maintained, not achieved once.
questions: Where is your life most out of harmony?
Which two areas of your life are actively working against each other right now, work hours wrecking your health, spending eating your runway?
If someone audited your calendar for a week, would they see one clear direction, or several competing ones?

definitions.def: How many months a business can keep operating before its cash runs out at the current rate of spending. Short runway means little time to fix problems before the money's gone.

content.equation_example: Business, health, home, relationships, finances, personal growth — 6 domains total. If 4 feel in sync: 4 ÷ 6 = 67% out of 100%. Higher means most areas reinforce each other; lower means multiple areas are actively working against each other.
content.closing_statement: Protect alignment as fiercely as you protect revenue.

caption:
Principle Nº 169: Life System Harmony

Everything must work together.

Ask yourself: Where is your life most out of harmony?
